report_name: Apple Patches Critical iOS Privacy Vulnerability (CVE-2026-28950) After FBI Recovers Deleted Signal Notifications

threat_actors.name: U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)
threat_actors.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) exploited CVE-2026-28950 to recover supposedly deleted Signal message notifications from a defendant's iPhone using forensic methods.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: Forensic analysis was used to recover improperly retained notifications, including message content, from a notification storage database on a suspect's iPhone.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-28950
vulnerabilities.description: A data handling flaw in Apple's iOS and iPadOS Notification Services that improperly retained copies of notifications from applications like Signal, even after they were deleted. This allowed for forensic recovery of sensitive data via local access to the device.

identities.name: Apple
identities.description: Vendor of iOS and iPadOS. Apple released emergency security updates to patch CVE-2026-28950, a vulnerability in the operating system's notification handling.

identities.name: Signal
identities.description: A secure messaging application whose notifications were improperly retained by iOS and iPadOS due to CVE-2026-28950. The vulnerability was not in Signal's encryption protocol.

indicators.name: iOS Deleted Notification Artifacts
indicators.pattern:
title: iOS Deleted Notification Artifacts
id: 9b1b3b1e-7b1e-4b1e-8b1e-7b1e3b1e7b1e
status: experimental
description: 'Detects the presence of the iOS notification database which may contain improperly retained notifications from deleted messages or apps on unpatched systems, as seen with CVE-2026-28950. This requires forensic analysis of the device filesystem.'
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/apple-fixes-bug-that-let-the-fbi-recover-deleted-signal-messages/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-24
tags:
  - attack.t1005
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains: '/private/var/mobile/Library/UserNotifications/db2/db'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: This indicator detects the presence of the iOS notification database file where improperly retained notifications were stored due to CVE-2026-28950. Detection requires forensic analysis of a device's file system.
